title: Boat Rental in Marbella: Costs, Seasons and What to Know in 2026
html:
Marbella offers some of the most glamorous boating on the Costa del Sol, with departures from Puerto Banús and prices typically ranging from €350 for a half-day RIB to €3,500 for a crewed Sunseeker. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local owners so you can compare verified boat listings and book with confidence.

Where to set off in Marbella?

The main departure points are Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino Marina, and Estepona Marina. Puerto Banús is the most famous, lined with superyachts and designer boutiques. Marbella Marina offers a more relaxed base with easy parking. Cabopino, east of Marbella, is quieter and ideal for heading towards the dunes of Artola. Estepona, a 20-minute drive west, has a charming old town and a well-sheltered marina. For an island trip, many boats from Marbella cross to Gibraltar waters or anchor off the coast of Sotogrande. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent?

Choose your vessel based on group size, desired speed and budget. Here are the most popular options for 2026: One option worth a look is the Sunseeker Predator 58 for hire.

  1. Semi-rigid RIB (6, 8 guests), ideal for a fast coastal tour or watersports. From €350 for three hours.
  2. Bowrider motorboat (8, 10 guests), perfect for a relaxed family day with swimming stops. From €500 for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran (10, 12 guests), stable, spacious and great for sunset cruises. From €1,200 for a full day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (12 guests), such as the Sunseeker Predator 58, offering style and speed. Check the Sunseeker Predator 58 for hire on BoatHire24. From €2,500 for a full day.
  5. Crewed sailing yacht (6, 8 guests), for a silent, wind-powered experience. From €800 for a half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices vary by boat type, season and duration, but expect to pay €350, €3,500 per outing. A shared sunset tour on a catamaran starts around €70 per person. A half-day on a small motorboat costs roughly €500. A full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 runs about €1,500. A crewed luxury yacht with skipper and fuel included can reach €3,500 in peak summer. Fuel is always extra unless stated, so ask your host for an estimate. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

  • Shared tour: €70, €100 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €350, €600
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200, €2,000
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (12 guests): €2,500, €3,500

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

No, you do not need a licence if you hire a boat with a skipper, which is the standard for most charters. For bareboat rentals (without a skipper) you must hold a recognised Spanish or international licence, such as the Spanish PER or an ICC with CEVNI endorsement. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book. High season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, making morning trips more comfortable. A local tip: anchor in the bay of Elviria for calm waters and a sandy beach less crowded than the main coast. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

Book a morning slot in July or August to avoid the afternoon Levante wind; the sea is calmest between 9 a.m. and 1 p.m.

How to book a boat through BoatHire24?

Using the platform is simple: search, compare and book directly with the owner. Start by using the boat search to filter by date, boat type and capacity. Each listing shows real photos, guest reviews and the total price including fees. Once you book, you can message the owner for arrival details. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €3,500 per outing
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 6, 12 passengers typical
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure points: Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a skippered boat without any licence, and that is the most common arrangement for tourists.

What is the best month for a boat trip in Marbella?

May and June offer warm weather, calm seas and fewer crowds, while September gives you the same heat with lower rates.

How many people can fit on a rental boat in Marbella?

Most skippered yachts and catamarans carry up to 12 guests, though larger vessels can accommodate more by arrangement.

Is it cheaper to book a boat online or at the marina?

Booking through BoatHire24 often gives you transparent pricing and verified reviews, sometimes at lower rates than walk-up rentals.

Whether you are planning a family outing or a corporate event, renting a boat in Marbella in 2026 is straightforward through BoatHire24. If you own a vessel, you can also rent out your boat and earn extra income via the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
